name: Quantum Random Number Generator
description: Generate 4 truly random bits using quantum superposition and measurement.
long_description: A quantum random number generator (QRNG) produces genuinely random bits by preparing qubits in superposition (|+⟩ = (|0⟩+|1⟩)/√2) and measuring. Unlike pseudo-random number generators, the randomness is intrinsic to quantum mechanics — no hidden pattern exists. This circuit generates 4 independent random bits simultaneously. Real QRNGs are commercially available and used in cryptography and scientific simulation.

references.title: Jennewein et al. (2000). A Fast and Compact Quantum Random Number Generator. Rev. Sci. Instrum. 71, 1675

author: OpenQC Community
